divergence_notes: Space: draft uses FC's concrete R^2 (EuclideanSpace R (Fin 2), the scoped notation from FormalConjecturesForMathlib/Geometry/2d.lean), house style of the sibling problems FC 982 and FC 1082; both hosted quantify over an abstract V with [NormedAddCommGroup V] [InnerProductSpace R V] [Fact (finrank R V = 2)]. Equivalent up to isometric isomorphism.

Convex position: draft uses FC's EuclideanGeometry.ConvexIndep (A : Set R^2) := forall a in A, a not in convexHull R (A \ {a}); hosted use Mathlib's ConvexIndependent R (Subtype.val : s -> V). Equivalent by Mathlib's convexIndependent_set_iff_not_mem_convexHull_diff.

Distinct-distance count: draft uses FC's EuclideanGeometry.distinctDistances (card of the image of dist over A.offDiag, a Nat); hosted build a Finset R of distances via (s.product s).filter (ne) |>.image dist and take .card at the theorem site. Same number.

Parametrisation: hosted carry (n : Nat) with h_card : s.card = n and conclude (distinctDistances s).card >= n / 2; draft states A.card / 2 <= distinctDistances A directly. Nat floor division renders the floor(n/2) in both; >= vs <= is orientation only. Same content, and byte-parallel to FC 1082.parts.i (Szemeredi's variant of this problem).

Cardinality floor: both hosted assume 3 <= n; draft keeps 3 <= A.card, reading 'form a convex polygon' as requiring at least three vertices. For <= 2 points the inequality is trivially true, so retaining the floor does not materially weaken the claim.
Hosted-vs-hosted: plby altman_erdos and jayyhk erdos_93 are identical statements modulo theorem name and a noncomputable marker on distinctDistances. No disagreement.
ams_rationale: AMS 52 (convex and discrete geometry): distinct distances among vertices of a convex polygon; matches FC 89/91/982.
